title: Question: Allowing users to expose other protocols (sctp) from the pod to the host
body:
Is there some way to expose other protocols such as "sctp" (rather than "tcp" and "udp") or is it left up to firewall rules? If you're using podman's command line, something similar to `$ podman -p $PORT:$PORT/sctp ...`

Doing a (very) quick search, it seems that this limitation might be referenced at https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/libpod/oci.go#L185. Is this where it's at and is the reason being that golang doesn't natively support sctp or some of the other protocols?

comments.body: Ah ok, awesome. Thanks! I'll take a gander over there then.
For reference, it looks like the CNI plugins do support this now, so we're adding support for SCTP to Podman now.
Good deal. Thanks for the heads up.
